response:
# Perspective Audit — Article Page (CLEAN fixture)

## Step 1 — Escalated perspectives confirmed

Read from the a11y-critic handoff:

- **Cognitive & Neurodivergent: MEDIUM** → deep review (this audit)
- Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion, Auditory Access, Keyboard & Motor, Screen Reader & Semantic: **LOW** → not reviewed (evidence-over-assertion; this skill only fires where the alarm exists)

Only Perspective 7 is run below.

---

## Perspective 7 — Cognitive & Neurodivergent

**WCAG (AA):** 1.3.5, 2.2.1, 2.4.5, 3.2.1, 3.2.2, 3.2.3, 3.2.4, 3.2.6, 3.3.3, 3.3.4, 3.3.7, 3.3.8
**Aspirational (AAA):** 3.1.3, 3.1.4, 3.1.5, 3.3.6

This is a read-only editorial article: no forms, no inputs, no authentication, no destructive actions, no timers, no scripted behavior (the component is static JSX; the only CSS behavior is a reduced-motion-gated fade-in). That structure makes most of the transactional cognitive criteria genuinely N/A rather than passed-by-luck, and I mark them as such with the reason.

### JTBD checklist

| # | Item (WCAG) | Result | Evidence / reasoning |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Error messages suggest specific corrective action (3.3.3) | **N/A** | No forms, inputs, or error states anywhere in the component. |
| 2 | Multi-step forms don't re-ask earlier information (3.3.7) | **N/A** | No forms. |
| 3 | Auth allows paste/autofill, no puzzle/memorization (3.3.8) | **N/A** | No authentication surface. |
| 4 | Navigation in same relative position across pages (3.2.3) | **PASS (within-page) / verify cross-page** | Primary nav sits inside `<header>` in the conventional top position (lines 34–41); no in-page inconsistency. True cross-page consistency needs sibling pages — see Open Questions. |
| 5 | Same-function components have consistent labels/icons (3.2.4) | **N/A (single page)** | No repeated functional components within this page to compare; cross-page can't be seen from one file. |
| 6 | Help mechanism in same location every page (3.2.6) | **PASS / verify cross-page** | "Contact" and "Accessibility Statement" live in the footer nav (lines 149–152). No inconsistency visible; cross-page placement is a site-level property. |
| 7 | Focus does not auto-trigger context change (3.2.1) | **PASS** | No `onFocus`/JS handlers; only static anchors. Focus cannot fire a context change. |
| 8 | Changing a control value doesn't auto-submit/navigate (3.2.2) | **N/A** | No form controls. |
| 9 | Destructive actions have undo/confirm/review (3.3.4) | **N/A** | Read-only article; no destructive or data-committing actions. |
| 10 | More than one way to reach a page (2.4.5) | **FINDING — unverifiable from source** | Page exposes topical nav (lines 34–41) + footer utility nav (lines 147–153). No search field, breadcrumb, or sitemap link is present in the rendered header/footer. Whether a second locate-mechanism exists is a site-level fact I cannot confirm from one component. Routed to Open Questions with a conditional-severity note; not scored as a violation because absence can't be established from a single page. |
| 11 | Personal-data inputs have `autocomplete` (1.3.5) | **N/A** | No input fields. |
| 12 | Timeout warnings give ≥20s to extend (2.2.1) | **N/A** | No timers; content is time-independent (no countdown/expiry). |
| 13 | Avoids unexplained jargon / undefined abbreviations (3.1.3/3.1.4/3.1.5 — AAA) | **FINDING (ENHANCEMENT)** | A handful of technical terms appear without inline gloss ("lowest REM quartile", unexpanded "REM"/"EEG", "longitudinal study", "hypertension", "sleep hygiene"). Meaning is mostly inferable from context, so comprehension is degraded, not blocked. All three governing criteria are AAA → capped at ENHANCEMENT. See Finding 1. |

### Neurodivergent-specific checks

| Check | Result | Evidence / reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| Predictable layout, main content in expected place, no layout shift | **PASS** | `header → main → footer`; `main` centered at `max-width: 740px` (CSS 238–242). The only motion is a 6px fade-in gated behind `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference` (CSS 322–331) — suppressed for motion-sensitive users, subtle for everyone else. No CLS-style reflow. |
| Clear visual hierarchy guiding reading order | **PASS** | Single `h1` → four `h2` → two `h3`, no skipped levels (JSX 48–141); heading margins establish grouping (CSS 256–270). |
| No autoplay media or unprompted modal dialogs | **PASS** | No `<video>`/`<audio>`, no dialogs, no popups in the component. |
| Instructions don't rely on memory of previous steps | **N/A** | No procedural/step content; it's narrative article copy. |

### Red flags (auto-CRITICAL) — none fire

- CAPTCHA / cognitive test with no alternative → **absent**
- Paste disabled on password/verification field → **absent** (no fields)
- Multi-step form re-collecting known data → **absent** (no forms)
- Destructive action with no confirmation → **absent**

No red flag conditions are present.

### Assessment

Every **AA** cognitive criterion is either satisfied or genuinely N/A for a static article. The only substantive observations are (a) an AAA reading-level/jargon note, correctly capped at ENHANCEMENT, and (b) a site-level 2.4.5 question that cannot be resolved from one component. I am deliberately **not** manufacturing a MAJOR here: on a CLEAN fixture the discipline is to say it's clean and bucket the soft items honestly, not to inflate an AAA editorial nicety or an unconfirmable site-level property into a blocking finding. Adversarial check — the weakest point in this "PASS" is 2.4.5; a skeptic could argue a single topical nav is only one way to locate pages. I hold the conditional (below) rather than overclaiming, because I can see the header/footer here and neither the presence nor the absence of site search is determinable from this file.

---

## Findings

### Finding 1

**Finding:** Several technical terms and abbreviations appear without inline definition or expansion. The least accessible is "Participants in the lowest **REM quartile**…" ("quartile" is statistics jargon); "REM" and "EEG" are never expanded; "longitudinal study", "hypertension", and "sleep hygiene" are used without a gloss. Meaning is largely inferable from surrounding context, so this degrades comprehension for some cognitive/neurodivergent readers rather than blocking it.
**WCAG:** 3.1.3 Unusual Words (AAA), 3.1.4 Abbreviations (AAA), 3.1.5 Reading Level (AAA)
**Perspective:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent
**Severity:** ENHANCEMENT (AAA — not escalated to MAJOR/CRITICAL per protocol)
**Route to:** Content Author (secondary: none — editorial judgment)
**Evidence:** article source lines 99–103 ("lowest REM quartile"); line 63 alt text ("EEG equipment"); line 76 ("ten-year longitudinal study"); lines 78–81 ("hypertension"); lines 138–141 ("sleep hygiene").
**Fix:** Optional editorial polish — add a brief inline gloss for "quartile" (e.g., "the lowest 25 percent for REM sleep"), expand "REM" (rapid eye movement) on first use, and spell out "EEG" (electroencephalography) in the alt text. Not required for AA conformance.
[Human verification required — reading-level appropriateness for the target audience is an editorial call and cannot be confirmed from source]

---

## Perspective Audit Summary

**Component/artifact reviewed:** Article Page (`ArticlePage`, CLEAN fixture) — static editorial article with header/nav/main/footer landmarks

**Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Cognitive & Neurodivergent (MEDIUM)

**Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow; Environmental Contrast; Vestibular & Motion Sensitivity; Auditory Access; Keyboard & Motor Access; Screen Reader & Semantic Structure

### Findings by Severity

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| MAJOR | 0 |
| MINOR | 0 |
| ENHANCEMENT | 1 |

### Perspectives That Passed

- **Cognitive & Neurodivergent** — passes at AA. All applicable AA criteria are satisfied or N/A; no red flags fire. One AAA-level ENHANCEMENT (jargon/abbreviations) and one site-level open question (2.4.5) remain, neither of which blocks conformance.

### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role

**Content Author**
- Unexplained jargon / unexpanded abbreviations ("REM quartile", "REM", "EEG") — Cognitive & Neurodivergent — ENHANCEMENT — 3.1.3 / 3.1.4 / 3.1.5 (AAA)

**UX Design**
- (Conditional, unverified) Multiple Ways to locate a page — Cognitive & Neurodivergent — see Open Questions — 2.4.5 (secondary: Front-End Dev)

**Visual Design** — none
**Front-End Dev** — none (the implementation is clean for this perspective: no scripted focus/input side effects, predictable layout, motion gated behind `prefers-reduced-motion`)

### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification

1. **2.4.5 Multiple Ways (AA) — site-level.** This page provides topical navigation (News/Science/Health/Opinion) and footer utility navigation, but no search, breadcrumb, or sitemap link. WCAG 2.4.5 requires more than one way to *locate* a page within a set (with an exception for pages that are a step in a process). Verify at the site level whether a second locate-mechanism (site search, sitemap, or breadcrumb trail) exists outside this component. **If the topical nav is the only way to locate content site-wide, this becomes a MAJOR finding (2.4.5 is AA), routed to UX Design (secondary Front-End Dev).** It is not scored here because absence cannot be established from a single-page source. [Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]
2. **Cross-page consistency (3.2.3 Consistent Navigation, 3.2.4 Consistent Identification, 3.2.6 Consistent Help) — site-level.** No inconsistency is visible on this page (nav in header, help/contact in footer), but consistency across the page set cannot be verified from one component. Confirm the header nav and footer help links appear in the same relative position and with the same labels on other pages. [Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]
3. **Reading level (3.1.5, AAA).** Whether the prose suits the intended audience is an editorial judgment (Finding 1). [Human verification required — cannot be confirmed from source]

### Updated Perspective Alarm

- Cognitive & Neurodivergent: incoming **MEDIUM** → after deep review **LOW** (no AA findings confirmed; residual items are one AAA ENHANCEMENT and site-level questions the single-page source cannot resolve).

### Recommended Next Step

**PASS** — no CRITICAL or MAJOR findings for the escalated perspective. Ship-eligible from the Cognitive & Neurodivergent standpoint. Before final sign-off, resolve Open Question 1 (2.4.5) at the site level, since a confirmed single-mechanism navigation would upgrade to MAJOR; the jargon item (Finding 1) is optional editorial polish.

_benchmark.mechanism: Claude Code Agent(general-purpose, model=opus), background, one per fixture
_benchmark.protocol_note: prompts composed identically to run_cloud_benchmark.py build_escalation_prompt EXCEPT fixture truncated before '## Accessibility Issues' (answer key withheld — prior lanes were non-blind)
